skill.name: Azure Web PubSub Service SDK for Python
skill.description: Azure Web PubSub Service SDK for Python enables real-time messaging, WebSocket connections, and pub/sub patterns at scale.
skill.summary: Build real-time applications with WebSocket connections using Azure Web PubSub Service.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: This is a documentation-only skill providing usage examples for the Azure Web PubSub Service SDK. No executable code is present. The skill references network operations and environment variable access as part of legitimate Azure SDK usage patterns. Static analysis found no security issues. The skill is safe to publish.

content.user_title: Build real-time apps with Azure Web PubSub
content.value_statement: Real-time communication is hard to scale. This skill helps you implement WebSocket-based pub/sub messaging using Azure Web PubSub Service.
actual_capabilities: Generate client access tokens for WebSocket connections with user authentication
Send messages to all clients, specific users, groups, or individual connections
Manage group membership for targeted message delivery
Check connection existence and close connections gracefully
Grant and revoke permissions for specific connections
Support both synchronous and asynchronous client operations
limitations: Requires Azure Web PubSub Service resource and connection string configuration
Client SDK requires separate package installation for WebSocket clients
Token-based authentication requires proper Azure credential setup
Does not cover advanced scenarios like serverless integration or event grid

use_cases.title: Live chat application
use_cases.description: Build a real-time chat system where users can send and receive messages instantly through WebSocket connections.

use_cases.title: Real-time dashboard updates
use_cases.description: Push live data updates to connected dashboard clients without polling, using group-based message routing.

use_cases.title: Multiplayer game networking
use_cases.description: Enable low-latency communication between game clients using WebSocket connections with user-based targeting.

prompt_templates.title: Basic setup
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me set up the Azure Web PubSub Service client in Python using a connection string. I need to send messages to all connected clients.
prompt_templates.scenario: Getting started with Azure Web PubSub

prompt_templates.title: User authentication
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a client access token for user 'alice' with permissions to join groups and send messages. Use Azure Entra ID authentication.

prompt_templates.title: Group messaging
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me how to add users to groups and send targeted messages to specific groups in Azure Web PubSub.

prompt_templates.title: Async broadcast
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an async function that broadcasts a JSON message to all connections and handles graceful shutdown of the client and credentials.

output_examples.input: Generate a client access token for user 'user123' with group membership
output_examples.output: Token generated with URL endpoint and user ID. User is assigned to groups 'group1' and 'group2' with sendToGroup and joinLeaveGroup roles.

output_examples.input: Send a notification to all connected clients
output_examples.output: Message broadcast successfully to all connections in the hub. Content type set to application/json with notification payload.

best_practices: Use roles to limit client permissions and follow the principle of least privilege
Generate short-lived tokens and implement token refresh mechanisms for security
Use group-based messaging for efficient targeted communication instead of broadcasting
anti_patterns: Using connection strings in source code instead of environment variables or Key Vault
Granting full permissions to all clients without role-based access control
Not handling connection failures or implementing reconnection logic in clients

faq.question: What is the difference between the Service SDK and Client SDK?
faq.answer: The Service SDK is for server-side operations like sending messages and managing connections. The Client SDK is for building WebSocket client applications that connect to the service.

faq.question: How do I authenticate users for WebSocket connections?
faq.answer: Use get_client_access_token with a user_id parameter. You can authenticate using connection strings or Azure Entra ID credentials.

faq.question: Can I send messages to specific users instead of broadcasting?
faq.answer: Yes, use send_to_user for individual users or send_to_group for multiple users in a group. This is more efficient than broadcasting.

faq.question: How do I handle connection disconnections?
faq.answer: Implement event handlers for connection events. Use connection_exists to check status before sending. Implement reconnection logic in client applications.

faq.question: What content types are supported for messages?
faq.answer: The SDK supports text/plain for simple strings and application/json for structured data. JSON is recommended for complex payloads.

faq.question: Is there an async version of the SDK?
faq.answer: Yes, import from azure.messaging.webpubsubservice.aio for async operations. This is recommended for high-throughput applications.
